_header.file_role: E1 anchor fleet AC baseline config (hand-written, wave 1 scaffold)

metadata.source_note: Qwen Qwen3-32B (base): 64 dense decoder layers, d_model 5120 with decoupled attention width (64 Q heads x head_dim 128 = 8192 inner, out-proj back to 5120; same decoupled-width pattern as configs/gpt_oss_120b.json), GQA 8 KV heads, SwiGLU FFN 25600, vocab 151936, RoPE theta 1000000 (rope_scaling null), RMSNorm eps 1e-6, untied embeddings. Architecture facts: official HF config.json of Qwen/Qwen3-32B (hidden_size 5120, num_hidden_layers 64, num_attention_heads 64, num_key_value_heads 8, head_dim 128, intermediate_size 25600, vocab_size 151936, rope_theta 1000000, rms_norm_eps 1e-6, max_position_embeddings 40960, tie_word_embeddings false); layer/head counts cross-checked against the Qwen3 Technical Report (arXiv 2505.09388, Table 1: Qwen3-32B 64 layers, 64/8 heads, 128K context). original_max_position set to 32768 (native pretrain context). Unrepresented in schema 0.3: per-head QK RMSNorm (q_norm/k_norm), ~16.8M params (~0.05%) not booked by AC's ledger; noted here, not modeled. params counts follow AC's parameter ledger (2*vocab*d embeddings + 2*d_model RMSNorm per layer; attention projections use n_heads*d_head independently of d_model, matching the Wave-18g decoupled-width support); for dense models the ledger books active == total, matching configs/mistral_7b.json conventions.
